This paper thoroughly designs a compression scheme to maximally utilize the GPU-friendly 2:4 fine-grained structured sparsity and quantization.
Specially, an original large model with dense weight parameters is first pruned into a sparse one by 2:4 structured pruning.
then the floating-point sparse model is further quantized into a fixed-point one by sparse-distillation-aware quantization aware training.
A mixed-strategy knowledge distillation is used during the pruning and quantization process.

Experiment results show GPUSQ-ViT scheme achieves state-of-the-art compression by reducing vision transformer models 6.4-12.7× on model size and 30.3-62× on FLOPs with negligible accuracy degradation on ImageNet classification, COCO detection and ADE20K segmentation benchmarking tasks.
Moreover, GPUSQ-ViT can boost actual deployment performance by 1.39-1.79× and 3.22-3.43× of latency.

Sparse GEMM is a specific type of matrix multiplication that accelerates the traditional dense GEMM by skipping unnecessary multiplications of redundant zeros. It provides 2x acceleration compared to dense GEMM.
Tensor Core is a hardware unit introduced in NVIDIA Volta GPU to accelerate matrix multiplication instructions. It has been enhanced to support sparse matrix multiplication in Ampere GPU.

Sparse GEMM imposes a constraint named 2:4 fine-grained structured sparsity on the allowed sparsity pattern, which requires two values from every four contiguous elements on rows to be zero. This constraint makes GPU hardware efficient for sparse matrix multiplication.

Quantization is another orthogonal technique in the model compression area, applying alternative formats other than standard 32-bit single-precision floating-point data type for weight parameters, inputs, and activations.
Quantization can significantly speed up model inference performance because low-precision formats have higher computational throughput support in many processors.
Low-precision representation in quantization helps to reduce the memory bandwidth pressure and can save memory-system operation time with cache utilization improvement.
Post Training Quantization (PTQ) [18] is a specific technique within the broader concept of quantization in model compression.

PTQ and QAT are two main strategies in quantization. PTQ directly calibrates on limited sample inputs to find the optimal clipping threshold and the scale factor to minimize the quantization noise. QAT inserts the quantization and de-quantization nodes into the float-point model structure, then undergoes the fine-tuning process to learn the scale factor adjustment with minimal influence on accuracy.

PTQ is preferred when without access to the whole training dataset. QAT inserts the quantization and de-quantization nodes into the float-point model structure, then undergoes the fine-tuning process to learn the scale factor adjustment with minimal influence on accuracy.

The ablation study of GPUSQ-ViT method focused on the loss adjustment factors and sparse-distillation-aware weight factors. Sparse-distillation-aware weight factor had an apparent boost for the compressed models' accuracy.
Enabling sparse-distillation-aware weight factor had a significant boost for the compressed models' accuracy. This effect was more influential on INT4 compared to INT8 models.

Disabling the weight factor for sparse distillation had a significant drop in INT4 compressed model accuracy. This suggests that the weight factor plays a crucial role in the quantization error from critical layers.
The aware weight factor indicates how much influence the quantization error from each critical layer has on the final accuracy.
